beats.narration_text: Halo. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of hydrogen radial probability density. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of hydrogen radial probability density

beats.narration_text: Hydrogen Radial Probability Density is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Radial probability density P(r) = r²|R. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Hydrogen Radial Probability Density

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: Hydrogen Radial Probability Density

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Hydrogen Radial Probability Density. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: x =. Verify: Radial probability density P(r) = r²|R. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Hydrogen Radial Probability Density. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: x =. Verify: Radial probability den

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Hydrogen Radial Probability Density — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from 07-hydrogen-radial-density.html

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Show the core physics loop from 07-hydrogen-radial-density.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: hydrogen radial probability density. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag x = from 0 to 1. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of x = from 0 to 1

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Slate: narrate slider sweep of x = from 0 to 1

beats.narration_text: Radial probability density P(r) = r²|R. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of hydrogen radial probability density. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Hydrogen Radial Probability Density simulation and add a second slider that controls x =. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Hydrogen Radial Probability Density simulation and add a second slider that controls x =. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Outro: Hydrogen Radial Probability Density · @Medhavy
